title: taraxacum procerisquameum
intro: Taraxacum procerisquameum, also known as the tall dandelion, is a plant species of the dandelion genus, characterized by its tall, upright growth habit and edible leaves. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum procerisquameum include its high content of vitamins A, C, and K, as well as its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support liver function, reduce blood pressure, and aid in digestion. Taraxacum procerisquameum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves and roots can be eaten raw or cooked in salads, soups, and stews.
